metadata.description: C&C 2026 poster submission — deadline 2026-04-16 (tomorrow AoE). Pick up where the prior session left off: confirm CFP requirements, refresh abstract with canonical numbers, build art-tech-tuned figures, compile submission package.

decision.description: Target ~2000 words for the C&C 2026 poster-track extended abstract, use placeholder title "Two Topics, 252 Artists: Measuring Compression in Public AI-Art Discourse (2013–2025)" (swappable), and position Ariya + user as co-first authors.
decision.rationale: Word count: 2000 fits one figure + methods paragraph + four compression phenomena + implications without padding (3000 allowed but poster-track reviewers expect compressed prose). Title: #2 is the sober-but-hooky safe pick; user flagged it may swap later. Authorship: consistent with Ariya as lead per user-memory.
decision.revision_note: User accepted the 2000-word target and authorship setup ("i agree with your recommendation on the word target"). Title held as placeholder ("lets go with one and then we might switch it").

context.conversation_snippet: User: "i agree with your recommendation on the word target and i am not yet sure about the title so lets go with one and then we might switch it.. start drafing"

contribution.description: C&C 2026 poster-track extended abstract: wrote main.tex (~1,850 words) in ACM manuscript-review format with anonymous authorship, four-phenomenon compression findings (concentration, frame redirection, binary simplification, voice collapse), style-matched controls, and implications for creative practice. US English, no em-dashes, no H1/H2/H3 markers, no map/territory metaphor, no keyword-based probe references.

context.conversation_snippet: User: "pick up where we left off with the poster template for C7C 2026 and tell me whatever other steps are needed" → "start drafing"

contribution.description: Poster-grade figures: fig1_concentration (horizontal bar chart showing 94.9% in 2 topics and 15 empty topics) and fig2_geometry (2D projection with absorbing-topic centroids and T13 "authenticity absorber" callout). Single script reads precomputed cache from figures/final_pipeline/figure_cache/, renders art-tech-tuned palette (coral #E85A4F for artists, slate #4A6FA5 for public).

context.conversation_snippet: User explicitly asked for "new versions of the figures that are more visually captivating and pleasing" (from POSTER_BRIEF).

contribution.description: Submission metadata document: captures portal path (PCS → SIGCHI → C&C2024 → Posters), form fields (title, abstract, keywords), anonymity checklist, word count confirmation (~1,850 < 3,000 limit), post-submission timeline (notification 2026-05-07, camera-ready 2026-05-21, conference 13–16 July 2026), and known-good state of files with warning about stale POSTER_PLAN.md and poster_abstract.tex.

Repo restructure and path-reference updates: moved jan_2026_manuscript/ACM2026/poster/ to manuscripts_and_presentations/poster/C_and_C_2026/, renamed jan_2026_manuscript/ to manuscripts_and_presentations/manuscript/. Updated .gitignore, CLAUDE.md (text + directory-tree diagram), HANDOFF_NEXT_SESSION.md (added restructure note at top), SESSION_CHANGES_20260403.md, POSTER_BRIEF_2026-04-15.md, and three memory files (MEMORY.md, project_cc_poster_task.md, project_submission_history.md). Updated scripts/generate_poster_figures.py OUT_DIR. Left 11 scripts/regenerate_*.py files and historical TRACE session logs untouched.

context.conversation_snippet: User: "i need you to actually move all of this work into its own 'poster/' dir that will be in 'manuscript_and_presentations' dir... update the .gitignore and update claude.md and handoff doc and any other documentations or logs that are necessary now based on changing paths"
